verses.commentary: Not many should become teachers. Teaching brings greater accountability. Teachers will be judged more strictly because they shape others' understanding of God. Responsibility increases with influence.

We all stumble in many ways. Universal sinfulness. But mastery over speech indicates maturity. The one never at fault in what they say is perfect (teleios, τέλειος), able to control the whole body. The tongue is the gauge of spiritual maturity.
Bits in horses' mouths control the whole animal. Small instrument, massive impact. The tongue is small but directs the entire person. What we say reveals what we are.
Ships are large and driven by strong winds, yet steered by a small rudder. The pilot controls the direction. So the tongue, though small, determines life's course. Words set trajectory.
The tongue is small but makes great boasts. A small spark sets a great forest on fire. Disproportionate power: tiny cause, massive destruction. Speech can ignite catastrophe.

The tongue is a fire, a world of evil. It corrupts the whole body and sets the course of life on fire, itself set on fire by hell (geenna, γέεννα). The tongue is a conduit of demonic destruction, spreading chaos and ruin.
All kinds of creatures have been tamed by humanity. Remarkable: humans tame wild beasts. Yet the tongue resists domestication. Sin's reach is comprehensive; no part of creation escapes its influence.

No human being can tame the tongue. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison. The tongue is untamable by human effort. Only divine transformation can control speech. Sin is a power beyond self-help.
With the tongue we praise God and curse people made in His image. Contradiction. Blessing and cursing from the same mouth. Speech reveals divided hearts. Worship and slander are incompatible.
Out of the same mouth come praise and cursing. This should not be. Incongruity between worship and wickedness. Genuine faith produces consistent speech. Hypocrisy is exposed by what we say.
Can fresh water and salt water flow from the same spring? Rhetorical question: no. Nature is consistent. Springs produce one kind of water. So should the heart produce one kind of speech.

Can a fig tree bear olives or a grapevine bear figs? No. Nature follows its kind. Neither can a salt spring produce fresh water. Fruit reveals the root. Speech reveals the heart. Consistency is evidence of authenticity.
Who is wise and understanding? Show it by good life and deeds done in humility. Wisdom is not intellectual; it is moral. True wisdom produces humble action. Knowledge without humility is arrogance.

Bitter envy and selfish ambition in your heart? Do not boast or deny the truth. Envy and ambition are antithetical to wisdom. Boasting about wisdom while harboring envy is self-deception. Pride blinds.

Such wisdom does not come from heaven. It is earthly, unspiritual, demonic. Three descending levels: earthly (worldly), unspiritual (psyuchikē, ψυχική, natural), demonic (daimoniōdēs, δαιμονιώδης). Wisdom rooted in envy is satanic in origin.
Where envy and selfish ambition exist, there is disorder and every evil practice. Envy produces chaos. Selfish ambition destroys community. Sin generates more sin. The fruit reveals the root.

Wisdom from heaven is first pure, then peace-loving, considerate, submissive, full of mercy and good fruit, impartial and sincere. Seven qualities of divine wisdom. Purity comes first; holiness precedes all other virtues. Heavenly wisdom produces peace, mercy, and integrity.

Peacemakers who sow in peace reap a harvest of righteousness. Peace is sown, righteousness is reaped. Kingdom agriculture: plant peace, harvest righteousness. Conflict produces chaos; peace produces godliness.
